pattern: A number's popcount equals the popcount of itself with the lowest set bit removed, plus one, so a single pass fills the table in linear time.
hints: The answer for index i is closely related to the answer for a smaller index you have already computed.
Dropping the lowest set bit of i with i & (i - 1) gives a strictly smaller number whose bit count you already know.
Set ans[0] = 0, then for each i from 1 to n let ans[i] = ans[i & (i - 1)] + 1.

edge_inputs.description: second statement example crossing a power of two
exactly a power of two boundary
one below a power of two has many set bits

stress.description: large upper bound exercising the full linear fill
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [max(0, min(100000, size))]
stress.note: deterministic single integer; size is the bound itself
